---
title: "All Grain Beer Recipes"
---

This is a work in progress to create a website to preserve [SoCo Homebrew All Grain recipes](https://socohomebrew.com/homebrew-recipe-kits/all-grain-recipe-kits/?page=1) before the store closes.

The recipes are saved as PDFs.

There are several organizations of the same recipes:

- [Recipes (Alpha)](01-recipes-alpha.qmd) is in alphabetical order by name.
- [Recipes (No Soco)](02-recipes-nosoco.qmd) is similar, but removes "SoCo" from recipe names.
- [Recipes (Filter)](03-recipes-filter.qmd) allows you to filter the list based on keywords to find things more quickly.
- [Recipes (Style)](04-recipes-by-style.qmd) organizes the recipes by beer style.

## Future work?

- I'd like the scrape the main information from the pdfs and store in a CSV.
    - UPDATE: This is mostly done, except I didn't capture "THE BOIL" portion that tracks extract additions.
- I want to make simple Quarto page for each recipe with good indexes that also include links to the original PDFs.
- And/or make a database.

## How this was done

- I used [Posit Assistant](https://posit-dev.github.io/assistant/) (an AI tool by posit built into RStudio) to build the scraper and pull all the pdfs. See the original prompt in [pa-scrape](pa-scrape.qmd). This yielded the file [x-extract-recipes](x-extract-recipes.qmd) that has the code that did the extraction. It took me about 5 minutes to write the prompt. It took working with PA another 15 minutes to get a test version of the scraper, and then 10 minutes for it to download them. This was done in a different account so I didn't save the full conversation.
- I used PA again to make most of the indexes. You can see the prompts in [pa-extract](pa-extract.qmd). I also saved the full conversation in the pa-conversations folder.
- I used PA again to fix some text on the indexes. [pa-indexes](pa-indexes.qmd) for prompt.
- I used PA to extract some key information from the PDFs to put into a csv. [pa-extract](pa-extract.qmd). It got most of the way there before I ran out of credits, so I did some cleanup in the file `data-processed/receipes.xlsx`.
